focus: PR 1 corpus path, PR 6 cache invalidation listener, PR 7 settings parity, PRs 8-10 benches, iter-1 adversarial self-check

title: PR-1 corpus repair missed a live legacy parity test and the requested export contract
reproduce: Stat the legacy spec-subfolder corpus path referenced by advisor-corpus-parity.vitest.ts:27-33; it is missing. Inspect python-ts-parity.vitest.ts:40 and note SPECKIT_BENCH_CORPUS_PATH is a local const, not exported.
fix: Export a single shared corpus-path constant/helper, repoint the legacy parity test to it, and run both parity suites.

title: PR-9 query-latency bench fail-softs missing baselines and internal bench errors as passing skips
reproduce: Temporarily rename code-graph-query-latency.baseline.json and run the PR-9 bench test; the baseline check throws inside the catch-all, returns skipped/passed, and the test accepts the skip.
fix: Fail on invariant failures such as missing or malformed baseline, zero samples, and extraction errors; reserve skip behavior for narrowly identified optional environment failures.

title: PR-5 query-latency freshness metric maps recent structural context to absent
reproduce: Create a code graph row with indexed_at between 5 and 60 minutes old, enable SPECKIT_METRICS_ENABLED=true, call buildContext(), and inspect spec_kit.graph.query_latency_ms; freshness_state is absent for recent data.
fix: Map recent explicitly to the intended PR-4 semantic bucket, likely live or stale, and reserve absent for no graph data.

evidence: startup-brief.ts:245-246 still maps freshness error to graphState missing, then startup-brief.ts:330 derives shared payload trustState through trustStateFromGraphState(graph.graphState).

Research-registry doc-scrub evidence exists, but it does not falsify the carried mismatch: plan.md:188 still lists mcp_server/tests/promotion-positive-validation-semantics.vitest.ts for deletion and the file remains present.
metrics.ts:578-589 still accepts arbitrary label maps; fusion.ts:320-322 and structural-indexer.ts:1419-1422 still feed env-derived labels without normalization.
